payload:
# PR Desk

Build a native macOS app called PR Desk.

## Product vision

PR Desk is an always-visible, calm desktop companion for GitHub pull requests.

It should feel like a native macOS desktop widget, similar to the Clock widget: always visible, draggable, lightweight, and glanceable.

The goal is to reduce GitHub/email checking and make it easy to act on PRs through Claude Code.

## Core UI

### Desktop widgets

Create two separate draggable desktop widgets:

1. **My PRs**
   - Shows PRs auth... [truncated, 3253 chars total]
Step 1 build verification: task-1778215146-5f09 (code-assist:pr-desk:step-01:build-verify) - Build PRDesk app with xcodebuild to verify project setup
build succeeded, app bundle verified at DerivedData

Task task-1778215146-5f09: Build succeeded but uses wrong window architecture. WindowGroup creates standard app windows, not desktop widgets. Must use NSPanel or custom NSWindow with floating level for always-visible draggable widgets. Foundation must be corrected before feature work.
Architecture fixed: WindowGroup replaced with FloatingPanel (NSPanel). App now uses borderless, translucent, floating panel with draggable behavior. Build succeeded.
Build verified, FloatingPanel architecture correct, app launches as desktop widget. Task task-1778215146-5f09 acceptance met.
Step 1 complete: FloatingPanel architecture verified. Ready for Step 2: GitHub Data Layer.
{"task_id": "task-1778215785-c96e", "task_key": "code-assist:pr-desk:step-02:fetch-my-prs", "description": "Implement gh CLI wrapper to fetch users authored PRs using gh pr list --author @me --json"}
task-1778215785-c96e: GitHubClient.fetchMyPRs() implemented - build succeeds, JSON parsing verified, empty array handling works
task-1778215785-c96e: Critical bug - gh pr list requires git repo context, fails outside git directory. Must use 'gh search prs' instead. Real harness test confirmed command fails with exit code 1.
task-1778215785-c96e: Fixed gh pr list repo context bug. Replaced with gh search prs. Build succeeds, integration test passes, works from any directory.
task-1778215785-c96e: gh search prs fix is correct, but hardcoded /opt/homebrew/bin/gh breaks portability. Use /usr/bin/env gh or check multiple paths.
Fixed hardcoded gh path. Now uses /usr/bin/env gh for portability across Mac architectures. Build succeeds, fetches 30 PRs correctly from non-git directory.
task-1778215785-c96e: All acceptance criteria met. Build succeeds, fetches PRs correctly from non-git directory using portable /usr/bin/env gh approach. Real harness verified. No bugs found.
task-1778215785-c96e closed. Step 2 has 3 open tasks remaining: fetch-tagged-prs, data-model, refresh-mechanism
task-1778215792-eb01: fetch-tagged-prs - Implement gh CLI wrapper for review-requested PRs

Implemented fetchReviewRequestedPRs() method in GitHubClient.swift. Uses gh search prs --review-requested=@me. Build succeeds, real harness verified 30 PRs fetched and parsed correctly from non-git directory.

task-1778215792-eb01 (fetch-tagged-prs): fetchReviewRequestedPRs() implemented correctly. Build succeeds, 30 PRs verified via real harness from non-git directory. All acceptance criteria met. No bugs found.
task-1778215792-eb01 (fetch-tagged-prs) closed successfully. Step 2 has 2 more ready tasks: data-model and refresh-mechanism.
task_id=task-1778215793-5304, task_key=code-assist:pr-desk:step-02:data-model, description=Add storage layer and data manager (PullRequest model already exists)
task_id=task-1778215793-5304, task_key=code-assist:pr-desk:step-02:data-model, event=Implemented PRDataStore with file-based JSON persistence. Build succeeds. Integration tests pass.

task_id=task-1778215793-5304, task_key=code-assist:pr-desk:step-02:data-model, Build verified. Integration test with real gh CLI passed. All acceptance criteria met: model created from gh JSON, persisted to file storage, retrieved successfully. Data integrity verified. No bugs found.
task-1778215793-5304 (data-model) closed. PRDataStore with file persistence complete. One ready task remains in Step 2: refresh-mechanism.
task-1778215794-1f61 (refresh-mechanism) - Step 2 final task. Coordinate GitHub fetch + persist. Accept: fetch both PR lists and save to PRDataStore.
PRRefreshService implemented. Fetches both PR lists concurrently and saves to PRDataStore. Wired to app launch. Build succeeds. Integration test verified 30 myPRs + 30 review PRs fetched and stored.
Build verified. Real harness passed: 30 myPRs + 30 reviewPRs fetched and stored. App launch wiring correct. Error handling verified. All acceptance criteria met. No bugs found.

Step 2 (GitHub Data Layer) complete. All 4 tasks closed: fetch-my-prs, fetch-tagged-prs, data-model, refresh-mechanism. Data layer verified: fetches 30+30 PRs, persists to Application Support, wired to app launch. Ready for Step 3 (Desktop Widget UI).

Step 3 wave created: 3 tasks for Desktop Widget UI (My PRs). First task: display-my-prs-list - Replace ContentView placeholder with real PR list from PRDataStore. Load myPRs, display compact list (title, repo, time), handle empty state. Task ID: task-1778218540-1c8e, Key: code-assist:pr-desk:step-03:display-my-prs-list

ContentView now displays real PR list from PRDataStore. Implemented PRListViewModel with reactive state. UI shows PR title, repo name, relative time. Handles loading/error/empty states gracefully. Build succeeds. 30 PRs verified in test harness.

CRITICAL BUG: Refresh button reloads stale disk data instead of fetching from GitHub via PRRefreshService. Fix: Wire refresh() to PRRefreshService.refresh() async call. MINOR: isLoading state is dead code (never shows). Task literal acceptance met but refresh UX is broken.

Fixed refresh button to fetch from GitHub. refresh() now calls PRRefreshService.refresh() async, then reloads from disk. Loading/error states properly handled. isLoading dead code removed from synchronous loadPRs(). Build succeeds.

Refresh button fix verified. Correctly fetches from GitHub via PRRefreshService.refresh(), handles async properly, shows loading states, verified with real harness. Build succeeds. All Critic issues resolved. task_id=task-1778218540-1c8e task_key=code-assist:pr-desk:step-03:display-my-prs-list
task-1778218540-1c8e complete. Step 3 continues: 2 ready tasks remain (highlight logic, window position persistence)

Step 3 wave continues: implement-highlight-logic (task-1778218543-ac6b) - Add visual highlighting for PRs needing action vs waiting. ContentView displays PR list; now distinguish action-needed PRs from waiting-for-reviewer PRs with visual state.

Implemented highlight logic for PRs. Added needsAction computed property (open non-draft = bright, draft/closed = dimmed). Updated PRRowView with conditional opacity and colors. Unit tests pass (3/3). Build succeeds. Visual states distinguishable. App launches successfully.

Over-engineered with double opacity layering (row opacity + color opacity). Requirement was simple dimming, but Builder added unnecessary progressive color variations. Creates multiplicative opacity (title=0.35, time=0.15) that's likely too dim. Violates KISS/YAGNI. Fix: use only row opacity for dimming, consistent colors for both states. Location: ContentView.swift:180-192

Fixed double opacity: removed color-level opacity variations, using only row opacity for dimming. Consistent color hierarchy (white, 0.6, 0.5) for both bright and dimmed states. Build succeeds, app launches successfully.
Opacity fix verified: single mechanism (row-level), no double layering, readable dimming (0.5 vs 0.35), simplified code. Build succeeds. All acceptance criteria met.
Highlight logic task complete. Step 3 has 1 remaining task: persist-window-position
Step 3 final task ready: persist-window-position (task-1778218545-401d)
task-1778218545-401d: Window position persistence implemented with save/restore and edge case handling (off-screen validation). Build succeeds, app launches.

task-1778218545-401d: Hardcoded UserDefaults key 'FloatingPanelPosition' in restoreSavedPosition() (line 93) duplicates positionKey constant (line 46). Change positionKey to static constant and use Self.positionKey in both savePosition() and restoreSavedPosition(). Otherwise if positionKey is changed, persistence silently breaks.
Fixed hardcoded UserDefaults key: moved positionKey to file-scope constant floatingPanelPositionKey, used in both savePosition() and restoreSavedPosition(). Build succeeds.
Fixed hardcoded key: file-scope constant floatingPanelPositionKey used by both save/restore methods. Build succeeds, DRY principle satisfied.
Step 3 complete: desktop widget with My PRs list, highlight logic, and position persistence. Build succeeds. All acceptance criteria met. Ready for Step 4.

Step 4 wave created: 3 tasks for second desktop widget (PRs I'm Tagged In). First task: create-second-widget (task-1778220617-785a). Build second FloatingPanel, position offset from first widget, verify both display simultaneously.

Two widgets created: parameterized ContentView with WidgetType enum, two FloatingPanelController instances in AppDelegate (myPRsPanel at x=100, reviewRequestedPanel at x=420). Build succeeds, app launches with both panels visible. Task: task-1778220617-785a

CRITICAL BUG: Both widgets share same UserDefaults key 'FloatingPanelPosition' and will overwrite each other's positions. On subsequent launches both widgets will restore to same position causing overlap. FloatingPanelController needs per-instance unique keys. Task: task-1778220617-785a, Key: code-assist:pr-desk:step-04:create-second-widget
Fixed position key bug - each panel now has unique UserDefaults key (MyPRsPanelPosition, ReviewRequestedPanelPosition). Build succeeds.
Position key bug fixed. Each panel has unique UserDefaults key (MyPRsPanelPosition, ReviewRequestedPanelPosition). Build succeeds. All acceptance criteria met.
task-1778220617-785a COMPLETE. Step 4 has 2 more ready tasks: display-tagged-prs, persist-second-widget-position. Build verified.
task-1778220623-ea88 (display-tagged-prs) ready. Second widget created. Now wire review-requested PR data to second widget with highlight logic.

task-1778220623-ea88 (display-tagged-prs) verified complete. Second widget displays review-requested PRs with highlight logic. All code paths exist: WidgetType.reviewRequested, loadReviewRequestedPRs, same layout, same highlight logic. Build succeeds. Test harnesses created.

task-1778220623-ea88 (display-tagged-prs) verified complete. Second widget displays review-requested PRs with same compact layout (title, repo, time) and highlight logic (needsAction). Build succeeds. Data flow verified: WidgetType.reviewRequested → loadReviewRequestedPRs() → PRListViewModel → ContentView. No critical bugs found.

Step 4 complete (desktop widgets for My PRs + PRs I'm Tagged In). All 3 tasks verified: create-second-widget, display-tagged-prs, persist-second-widget-position. Both widgets display with independent position persistence. Build succeeds. Next: Step 5 - Full Detail Window (4/8 steps complete, 50% of plan)

Step 5 - Full Detail Window started. First task: create-detail-window (task-1778221994-88dd). Create NSWindow-based detail window with tabs for My PRs and PRs I'm Tagged In. Build must succeed and window must be showable programmatically.
DetailWindow implementation complete: NSWindow-based controller with TabView for My PRs and PRs I'm Tagged In. Wired to AppDelegate. Build succeeds. Task: task-1778221994-88dd
DetailWindow has double tab UI (manual Picker + TabView both render tab bars). Remove Picker, keep only TabView. Task: task-1778221994-88dd, Key: code-assist:pr-desk:step-05:create-detail-window
Fixed double tab UI - removed redundant Picker, TabView now provides single native macOS tab bar. Build succeeds.
task-1778221994-88dd: Double tab UI fix verified - removed redundant Picker, single native TabView remains. Build succeeds. All acceptance criteria met.

task-1778221994-88dd (create-detail-window) complete. DetailWindow with native macOS tabs created, wired to AppDelegate, build succeeds. Step 5: 1 of 3 tasks done. Next: task-1778221995-f561 (display-detailed-pr-rows) now ready.

task-1778221995-f561 (display-detailed-pr-rows) ready. Replace MyPRsDetailView and ReviewRequestedDetailView placeholders with real PR lists showing detailed columns (title, number, repo, status, activity, usernames, Open in Claude button). Use PRDataStore. Follow TDD.

task-1778221995-f561 complete. DetailRowView displays all fields (number, title, repo, status, activity, author, Open in Claude button). MyPRsDetailView and ReviewRequestedDetailView load from PRDataStore. Highlight logic applied. Build succeeds.

task-1778221995-f561 complete. DetailRowView displays all fields (number, title, repo, status, activity, author, Open in Claude button). MyPRsDetailView and ReviewRequestedDetailView load from PRDataStore. Highlight logic applied. Build succeeds. No bugs found.

task-1778221995-f561 complete. DetailRowView displays all fields (number, title, repo, status, activity, author, Open in Claude button). MyPRsDetailView and ReviewRequestedDetailView load from PRDataStore. Highlight logic applied. Build succeeds. Step 5 has 1 more task (wire-widget-click). Steps 6-8 remain.

task-1778221996-31c6 (wire-widget-click) ready. DetailWindow created with tabs. DetailRowView displays all fields. PRDataStore integration working. Need to wire PRRowView click handler to open DetailWindow with selected tab. Final task in Step 5 wave.

task-1778221996-31c6 (wire-widget-click) implemented. PRRowView has onTapGesture that posts notification with widgetType. AppDelegate observes notification and shows DetailWindow with correct initial tab. Build succeeds.

task-1778221996-31c6: Window not closed before reassignment (critical bug). Required fix: Add detailWindow?.close() before reassignment in showDetailWindow(). Eliminates redundant if/else and prevents multiple windows/memory leaks.
task-1778221996-31c6: Fixed window close bug - detailWindow?.close() before reassignment
task-1778221996-31c6: Critical window close bug fixed, all acceptance criteria met, build succeeds
Step 5 complete - wire-widget-click closed, all 3 tasks done. Demo: clicking widget opens detail window with correct tab. Next: Step 6 wave creation.
Step 6 wave created: 3 tasks for Claude integration. Starting task-1778223832-e74a (claude-button-handler). Button action handler, PR metadata extraction, build verification.

task-1778223832-e74a complete. openInClaude() extracts PR metadata (number, title, repo, URL). Console logging for verification. Button clickable. Build succeeds. Test harness: TestClaudeButtonHandler.swift.

task-1778223832-e74a approved. openInClaude() correctly extracts all 4 PR metadata fields (number, title, repo, URL). Build succeeds. No bugs found. Test harness provides manual verification. Ready for Finalizer.
task-1778223832-e74a closed. Step 6 progress: 1/3 tasks complete. Next ready: task-1778223833-6d2c (generate-claude-prompt), task-1778223834-1138 (launch-terminal-claude)

task-1778223833-6d2c (generate-claude-prompt) ready for Builder. Step 6: 1/3 complete. Implement prompt generator for Claude Code integration based on PR type (My PRs with comments vs PRs I'm tagged in).

task-1778223833-6d2c: Claude prompt generator implemented. Generates actionable prompts for My PRs (address feedback) and Review Requested (provide review) contexts. Uses available PR data + suggests gh CLI commands. Build succeeds.
task-1778223833-6d2c: Claude prompt generator passed review. All acceptance criteria met. Prompts are actionable, context-rich, aligned with product vision. Build succeeds. Low risk implementation.
task-1778223833-6d2c (generate-claude-prompt) closed and finalized. Step 6: 2 of 3 tasks complete. 1 ready task remains: launch-terminal-claude. Overall plan: 5 of 8 steps complete.

task-1778223834-1138 (launch-terminal-claude) ready for Builder. Task: Launch Terminal and execute Claude Code with context. Step 6: 2 of 3 tasks complete (claude-button-handler ✅, generate-claude-prompt ✅). Overall plan: 5 of 8 steps complete.
task-1778223834-1138 (launch-terminal-claude) complete. TerminalLauncher implemented with AppleScript, wired to DetailWindow button. Build succeeded. Ready for Critic verification.

task-1778223834-1138 (launch-terminal-claude): CRITICAL BUG - escapeForAppleScript missing newline escaping. Multi-line prompts from ClaudeIntegrationService will break AppleScript string literal. Fix: add .replacingOccurrences(of: "\n", with: "\\n") and .replacingOccurrences(of: "\r", with: "\\r") to escapeForAppleScript(). See scratchpad for full analysis.
task-1778223834-1138 (launch-terminal-claude): Fixed critical newline escaping bug. Added \n, \r, \t escaping to escapeForAppleScript(). Build succeeds. Multi-line prompts now work correctly.
task-1778223834-1138 (launch-terminal-claude): Fix verified. Newline/tab/CR escaping correct. All adversarial tests pass. Build succeeds. Step 6 ready for finalization.
Step 6 (Claude Integration) complete - all 3 tasks closed. Remaining: Step 7 (Polling/State), Step 8 (Local Repo Config). Ready for next wave creation.
Step 7 wave created: 3 tasks for Polling and State Management. First task: implement periodic polling mechanism using Timer (task-1778225447-5ca1)
task-1778225447-5ca1: Periodic polling mechanism implemented with Timer, 5-minute interval, lifecycle integration, and logging
task-1778225447-5ca1: Periodic polling implementation passed review - all acceptance criteria met, no critical bugs
task-1778225447-5ca1 (periodic-polling) complete - Step 7 has 2 more ready tasks: update-highlights and handle-edge-cases
task-1778225449-c1eb (update-highlights) ready for Builder - Wire PRDataStore updates to ContentView refresh using NotificationCenter/Combine
task-1778225449-c1eb: NotificationCenter wiring complete, both widgets auto-refresh on data change

DetailPRListViewModel missing notification observer - detail window will not auto-refresh on data changes. Need to add same observer pattern as PRListViewModel (lines 40-54 in ContentView.swift) to DetailPRListViewModel in DetailWindow.swift
DetailPRListViewModel notification observer added - detail window will now auto-refresh on data changes
DetailPRListViewModel notification observer added - all UI components now reactive - build succeeds - confidence 95/100
task-1778225449-c1eb finalized - notification mechanism complete - Step 7: 2 of 3 tasks done - next: handle-edge-cases
task-1778225450-661b: handle-edge-cases - network failures, rate limits, stale data UI - Step 7 final task
task-1778225450-661b - edge case handling complete - last update time tracking, stale data UI, error handling - build succeeds
task-1778225450-661b - exponential backoff for rate limits not implemented - task explicitly requires exponential backoff but only basic error logging exists - confidence 85/100
task-1778225450-661b - exponential backoff implemented - 5min→10min→20min→40min→60min progression - resets on success - build succeeded
task-1778225450-661b - exponential backoff verified - formula correct: 5→10→20→40→60min - state tracking sound - timer restart safe - last update time tracking verified - all acceptance criteria met
Step 7 complete (polling with exponential backoff, UI updates, edge case handling). Step 8 - Local Repo Config remains. Build: SUCCESS. Runtime tasks: 0 open.

## Reason
max_iterations

## Status
Stopped at iteration limit.

## Summary
- Iterations: 100
- Duration: 3h 24m 51s
- Exit code: 2
